city in and state capital of North Carolina, United States of America
description_wiki: Raleigh (  RAH-lee) is the capital city of the U.S. state of North Carolina.

description: American university
description_wiki: Shaw University is a private historically Black university in Raleigh, North Carolina.

description: 1861–1865 civil war in the United States between the North and the South over the enslavement of African-Americans
description_wiki: The American Civil War (April 12, 1861 – May 26, 1865; also known by other names) was a civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened because of the election of Abraham Lincoln and the growing abolitionist movement in the North.

description: city in Beaufort County, North Carolina, United States of America
description_wiki: Washington is a city in and the county seat of Beaufort County, North Carolina, United States, located on the northern bank of the Pamlico River.

aliases: State of Rhode Island and Providence Plantations
description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Rhode Island (  ROHD) is a state in the New England region of the Northeastern United States.

description: 1862 Confederate ironclad
description_wiki: CSS Virginia was the first steam-powered ironclad warship built by the Confederate States Navy during the first year of the American Civil War.

description: city in Wayne County, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Goldsboro, originally Goldsborough, is a city in and the county seat of Wayne County, North Carolina, United States.

Union Army general (1817–1873)
description_wiki: Edward Richard Sprigg Canby (November 9, 1817 – April 11, 1873) was a career United States Army officer and a Union general in the American Civil War.

description: North Carolina politician (1818-1892)
description_wiki: William Woods Holden (November 24, 1818 – March 1, 1892) was an American politician who served as the 38th and 40th governor of North Carolina.

description: United States Navy officer
description_wiki: Worth Bagley (April 6, 1874 – May 11, 1898) was a United States Navy officer during the Spanish–American War, distinguished as the only U.S. naval officer killed in action during that war.

description: U.S. Navy federal service academy in the Annapolis, Maryland area
description_wiki: The United States Naval Academy (USNA, Navy, or Annapolis) is a federal service academy adjacent to Annapolis, Maryland.

description: army of the Confederate States
description_wiki: The Confederate States Army (CSA), also called the Confederate army or the Southern army, was the military land force of the Confederate States of America (commonly referred to as the Confederacy) during the American Civil War (1861–1865), fighting against the United States (also known as Union) forces to support the rebellion of the Southern states and uphold and expand the institution of slavery.

aliases: Presbyterian Church in the United States
United Presbyterian Church in the U.S.A.
description: Mainline Protestant denomination in the United States
description_wiki: The Presbyterian Church (USA), abbreviated PCUSA, is a mainline Protestant denomination in the United States.

description: group of historically related denominations of Protestant Christianity
description_wiki: Methodism, also called the Methodist movement, is a Protestant Christian tradition whose origins, doctrines and practice derive from the life and teachings of John Wesley.

aliases: Democratic Party of the United States
description: American political party
description_wiki: The Democratic Party is the major liberal political party in the United States, sitting on the center to center-left of the political spectrum.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Louisiana (French: Louisiane; Spanish: Luisiana [lwiˈsjana]; Louisiana Creole: Lwizyàn) is a state in the Deep South and South Central regions of the United States.

description: American politician (1859-1912)
description_wiki: Charles Brantley Aycock (November 1, 1859 – April 4, 1912) was the 50th governor of the U.S. state of North Carolina from 1901 to 1905.
